meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Finish Your Student Projects Fast
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on your code. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to debug complex errors, master your workflow, and finally ship your first professional project.
content.answer_block: The 'can't finish' cycle stems from getting stuck on blockers that kill momentum. By integrating AI-driven debugging, you externalize the cognitive load of error resolution. Using tools like Antigravity and Claude Code turns insurmountable bugs into actionable learning milestones, allowing you to bypass frustration and maintain the velocity required to ship.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks architecting your dream app, but now you’re staring at a red console log that feels like a brick wall. The excitement has evaporated, replaced by the crushing weight of 'tutorial hell' and unfinished repositories. Every developer has been here—the point where the complexity outpaces your current skill, and the project begins to gather digital dust.

This isn't a failure of your intelligence; it's a failure of your tooling. By leveraging AI-native debugging, you transform these blockers from project-killers into elite learning opportunities. We aren't just fixing bugs; we are building the mental model required to transition from a student coder to a professional software engineer.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: Before throwing code at an AI, you must define the scope of the failure. Use AI to scan your logs to identify the exact line or dependency causing the crash.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze these terminal logs and trace the execution path to the specific function causing the unhandled promise rejection.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read logs/error.log --instruction 'Identify the root cause of this stack trace.'

steps.title: Generate a Minimal Reproducible Example
steps.description: AI is most effective when it sees a isolated snippet. Extract the failing logic into a separate file to ensure the AI isn't distracted by extraneous code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a minimal reproduction of this component's state management bug in a new isolated test file.

steps.title: Iterative Hypothesis Testing
steps.description: Don't just ask for a fix. Ask for three potential architectural solutions and evaluate them based on scalability and performance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide three architectural patterns to solve this race condition. Rank them by ease of implementation and long-term maintainability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Compare these 3 approaches for fixing the race condition in the auth flow.'

steps.title: Automated Refactoring
steps.description: Once the fix is identified, let the AI apply the changes across your codebase to ensure type safety and consistent style.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the identified logic and propagate the changes to all dependent modules, ensuring TypeScript interfaces remain valid.

steps.title: Implement Regression Testing
steps.description: Ensure the bug never returns. Use AI to write a unit test that specifically targets the edge case you just resolved.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Jest test case that covers this specific edge case to ensure it doesn't regress in future deployments.

pro_tips: Always ask the AI to explain 'why' a fix works before applying it; treat the AI as a senior mentor, not a code generator.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to provide context about your project's specific coding standards and preferences.
When stuck, force a 'rubber duck' session by asking the AI to summarize your logic back to you in plain English.
Keep your context window clean by deleting large unused files or logs before initiating a deep debugging session.
Integrate a 'Fix-Verify-Commit' loop: never move to the next feature until the AI has verified the current fix passes all existing tests.
content.next_steps: Once your project is stable, focus on deployment. Start by automating your CI/CD pipeline so you can ship incremental updates without manual intervention.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools to turn your unfinished repos into a high-impact portfolio. Stop staring at errors and start shipping features that showcase your engineering potential. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

AI is most effective when it sees a isolated snippet. Extract the failing logic into a separate file to ensure the AI isn't distracted by extraneous code.
Don't just ask for a fix. Ask for three potential architectural solutions and evaluate them based on scalability and performance.
Once the fix is identified, let the AI apply the changes across your codebase to ensure type safety and consistent style.
Ensure the bug never returns. Use AI to write a unit test that specifically targets the edge case you just resolved.
